claims.text: Majestic 12 was established in 1947 to take charge of crashed UFOs and their biological and technical remains.

evidence.quote:
Majestic 12 was established in 1947
to take charge of the technic

claims.rationale: MJ-12 is from a set of documents that first surfaced via Timothy Good and Bill Moore in the 1980s. The FBI formally declared the core Eisenhower briefing documents to be fabrications; Stanton Friedman defended some. The organization's existence has never been independently confirmed by declassification or credible whistleblower record; skeptical consensus treats it as hoax. Low directTruth reflects this.

claims.text: According to the Project Looking Glass narrative, Sumerian cylinder seals given by the Anunnaki encoded instructions for accessing naturally occurring wormholes or Stargates.

evidence.quote:
Anunnaki arrived on Earth 500,000
years ago seeking gold to repair their
planet's atmosphere

claims.rationale: Zecharia Sitchin's Anunnaki-as-ancient-astronauts thesis has been uniformly rejected by Assyriologists and historians (Michael Heiser has a detailed published refutation). Cylinder seals are real Sumerian artifacts but contain ordinary administrative/religious imagery; no wormhole instructions have been identified by academic epigraphers. directTruth very low.

claims.text: Project Looking Glass was a machine that allowed operators to see the past, present, and probabilistic futures.

evidence.quote:
Looking Glass machine allowed the
user to see past present and future
seeing the past is interesed but seeing
th

claims.rationale: Project Looking Glass is a claim unique to Dan Burisch's testimony (and relatives Bill Hamilton, Bill Uhouse). No physicist, declassified document, or external whistleblower has corroborated a working chronoscope. The CIA's Star Gate remote-viewing program (1978-1995) is real and declassified; it did not involve time viewing through a wormhole machine.

claims.text: Looking Glass operators reportedly saw nothing past December 21st, 2012 — the date the Mayan calendar ends — and all timelines converged on that date.

evidence.quote:
past 2012 specifically
December 21st 2012 the Mayan calendar
ends on that date all timelines
converged on

claims.rationale: The 2012 Mayan-calendar apocalypse passed uneventfully. The supposed prophetic blackout was falsifiable and falsified by the passage of time. Maya scholars (David Stuart, et al.) repeatedly explained that the long-count b'ak'tun reset is not an apocalypse prediction. Narrative element of a debunked 2012 mythology.

dependencies.rationale: Requires Looking Glass to be a real operating device; if it isn't, the 'can't see past 2012' result is vacuous.

claims.text: The supposed 2012 disaster — a massive geophysical pole shift of almost 5° — never occurred.

evidence.quote:
December 21st 2012 a disaster took place
there was a massive geophysical pole
shift of almost 5° the shift was caused
by the Ea

claims.rationale: Host presents the pole-shift scenario as 'a different timeline' event to set up the story, but no geophysical pole shift occurred on Dec 21, 2012 — the earth's axis, magnetic pole motion, and seismic record show nothing of the kind. USGS, NASA, and IGS data all confirm normal baselines.

dependencies.rationale: This describes the specific catastrophe supposedly at the vanishing point in the Looking Glass visions.

claims.text: The Greys are not aliens but future humans (p45s) from 45,000 years in the future, evolved underground after a cataclysm.

evidence.quote:
ibed as
the Grays the Grays we see now aren't
aliens they're us 45,000 years in the
future Majestic 12 calls t

claims.rationale: Future-human-Greys is a specific claim from Dan Burisch's narrative. No independent evidence for Greys at all, let alone evolutionary-descendant-Greys from a failed timeline. Combines time travel, mass underground evolution, and a specific future date — extraordinary claim with zero verifiable anchor.

claims.text: The p-45 Greys are deliberately pushing humanity toward a cataclysm in 2012 (now delayed to 2030) so that they can come into existence.

evidence.quote:
p45 Grays wanted that
cataclysm to happen in 2012 they still
want it they need it to happen so they
can

claims.rationale: Self-undermining: if future Greys need the cataclysm to happen for them to come into existence, and it didn't happen in 2012, then by the narrative's own logic the Greys currently abducting people shouldn't exist — a grandfather-paradox contradiction the host doesn't resolve. No external evidence.

dependencies.rationale: [logical] They 'need' the cataclysm to have happened in 2012 for them to exist, but it didn't happen — yet the narrative says they still exist and are pushing for it in 2030, which is incoherent.

claims.text: Dan Burisch claimed a PhD from Stony Brook University in 1990, but Stony Brook has no record of him.

evidence.quote:
itary with the rank of Captain
but there's no record of that he said he
got his PhD in 1990 from Stony Brook
University in New York when Stony Brook
said they n

claims.rationale: This is a directly verified fact — Stony Brook University's registrar has denied having any record of Dan Burisch/Crain. Documented in Kevin Randle's investigations and UFO Watchdog reporting. Established fact about Burisch's credentials claim.

dependencies.rationale: [undercuts] If Burisch's core credentials are fabricated, his source-of-all-this-information status for Project Looking Glass is severely damaged.

claims.text: During the years Dan Burisch claimed he was working at the secret S4 lab near Area 51, official records show he was actually working as a parole officer in Las Vegas.

evidence.quote:
parole officer in
Las Vegas there's documentation t

claims.rationale: Corroborated by investigative reporters (notably KLAS-TV's George Knapp). Burisch's Nevada state employment as a parole officer is a matter of public record. Established fact contradicting his S4 lab narrative.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] Being a full-time parole officer in Las Vegas is incompatible with full-time classified S4 lab work, removing Burisch's firsthand access to the Looking Glass narrative.

claims.text: Bob Lazar publicly denies knowing Dan Burisch, saying 'anyone who believes this guy should be ashamed of themselves.'

evidence.quote:
Bob Lazar Lazar
said anyone who believes this guy should
be ashamed of themselves he said he
never met this knucklehead kn

claims.rationale: Lazar's dismissal of Burisch is well-documented in multiple interviews (Joe Rogan, Jeremy Corbell documentary). Lazar has consistently distanced himself from Burisch's Looking Glass/Majestic-12 claims. Widely reported.

dependencies.rationale: [undercuts] When a fellow Area-51 whistleblower whose story Burisch builds on calls Burisch a fraud, Burisch's version loses its main corroborating voice.

claims.text: The Vatican chronovisor allegedly developed by Father Pellegrino Ernetti in the 1950s is not a Looking Glass device — the photos supposedly taken with it are proven fakes.

evidence.quote:
ven took
pictures of Christ using the chronovisor
now I can't prove the chronovisor is
fake but I do

claims.rationale: The 'Christ' photograph attributed to Ernetti was identified in 1994 as a photograph of a wooden sculpture by Cullot Valera at the Sanctuary of Merciful Love in Collevalenza. Published in Italian news and La Stampa. Host explicitly concedes the fakery. Well-documented.

claims.text: The host's verdict: Burisch's story is farfetched and contradicts itself (branching timelines vs. single timeline, undocumented credentials, built on already-debunked foundations like the chronovisor and Chan Thomas's Adam and Eve).

evidence.quote:
Dan has added a lot of Science Fiction
tropes he's jammed so many details into
his story t

claims.rationale: Host's summary judgment. Omitting directTruth because 'farfetched' is a synthesis, not a specific falsifiable claim — though each component (credentials fraud, chronovisor fake, Adam-and-Eve debunk) is independently established above.

dependencies.rationale: The credentials-fabrication point is one pillar of the overall skeptical verdict.
The parole-officer evidence is another pillar.
The chronovisor-photos-are-fake finding damages a source Burisch's backers cite.
